title: Oldenlandia spermacocoides
taxonomy: Oldenlandia spermacocoides is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The species Oldenlandia spermacocoides falls under the genus Oldenlandia, which comprises approximately 100 species of flowering plants. The classification of Oldenlandia spermacocoides is further divided into the subfamily Rubioideae. The species is native to tropical regions of Asia.
uses: Oldenlandia spermacocoides is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. In some parts of the world, it is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves are also said to have antibacterial and antifungal effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin infections.
